repos/ops.reason: Merged ops#38 Gemma 4 Ollama memory sizing fix

regressions_found.name: Ollama memory limit too low for live gemma3:4b inference
regressions_found.symptom: Direct gemma3:4b run and spike auto-troubleshoot Ollama call failed with HTTP 500: model requires more system memory (4.0 GiB) than is available (3.9 GiB)
regressions_found.fix: Raised AI-OS Ollama default resources to request 3Gi and limit 5Gi, changed default model comments/value to gemma3:4b, merged as noetl/ops#38, and patched the local ollama deployment to the same sizing.
regressions_found.post_fix_evidence: Direct ollama run gemma3:4b succeeded; spike execution 620356925057663015 GREEN with diagnosis source=ollama and model evidence in diagnose execution 620356943059615814.

iterations: Synced noetl/ops/e2e/docs/cli/gui to main and verified noetl v2.35.9 release tag matches HEAD.
Probed GHCR manifest for ghcr.io/noetl/noetl:v2.35.9 using anonymous bearer flow; manifest available.
Ran bump_image lifecycle for v2.35.9; components were already unchanged and rollouts healthy.
Re-registered bump_image, diagnose_execution, and spike_e2e_test catalog playbooks.
Verified gemma3:4b was installed but initial live Ollama inference failed due to 4Gi memory limit.
Landed ops#38 and patched local ollama deployment to 5Gi limit / 3Gi request.
Re-ran spike and captured Gemma 4 model evidence from the diagnose sub-execution.
Ran all five prepared smokes successfully.
Ran full 8-bucket sweep: cluster health, spike stability x3, MCP JSON-RPC, bump_image idempotent, auto-troubleshoot contract, catalog re-register, and failed-sub envelope shape.
notes: The stack is current and clean after one in-flight ops fix. Verdict is AMBER because a deployment regression surfaced and was fixed during the sweep. The final deployed NoETL tag is v2.35.9 for server, worker, and ollama-bridge.
